scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein); build_insns 13 vs target 16 (3 short)

sessions.lever: removed 2 pins (p asm $3, volatile ret asm $2) + made 5 regfix rules unnecessary; three-return form -> p in $v1, ret in $v0, frameless, naturally

reviewer.evidence: Candidate is the natural three-return form: 0 cheat-asm, 0 rules, no dead store. Registers and framelessness fall out for free. Not byte-identical (3 short) — GCC folds the boolean. Clean WIP, not committed.

remaining_gap.summary: Target keeps a verbose `andi v0,v0,1; bnez v0,end; li v0,1; move v0,zero` for the second test (returns 1 if p[0]&1 else 0). GCC folds `if(p[0]&1) return 1; return 0;` -> `return p[0]&1;` (boolean simplification in jump-opt), emitting `andi; jr; nop` (3 fewer insns). p->$v1 and ret->$v0 already MATCH in the candidate. The frame is already removed (frameless leaf). So the gap is ONLY the boolean if-to-return fold.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD matched via 2 pins + a forbidden `ret=1; ret=0` dead-store trick + 5 regfix (frame-removal subu->nop @1, delete @13, $4->$2 renames @2/4/5). The candidate retires ALL of that EXCEPT it can't reproduce target's unfolded branch — GCC always folds the 0/1 boolean.

next_hypotheses: Defeat the `if(bool) return 1; return 0` -> `return bool` fold WITHOUT a dead store. Shared-end-label (tried: still folds, ret is 0 in 2 of 3 paths so GCC sees ret=p[0]&1). do-while-zero exception is for reorg.c branch-inversion, NOT this jump-opt boolean fold (likely doesn't apply). Investigate the exact GCC pass that folds it (jump.c if-to-return / cse) and whether any natural C form blocks it.

If no clean form reproduces target's verbose branch, this is genuinely not pure-C-closable (target was hand-written or from a non-foldable source) -> canonical-asm auth or documented plateau. The candidate floor-3 is the honest pure-C limit.
decomp-permuter from candidate.c (tiny fn) — may find a non-folding spelling.

rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN dead store (register-alloc-pure-c Lever D). It defeats the fold by writing a dead `ret=1` -> `li v0,1`, but that's the exact coercion the policy forbids. Do not re-derive.

rejected_forms.form: shared-end-label goto form (ret var, all paths goto end)
rejected_forms.reason: Same floor as three-return but no better — GCC still folds (ret is 0 in 2 of 3 paths). Three-return is cleaner.
Folds identically. No effect vs the bare three-return.
